name: Valu + Pawn
description_short: FirstCash is the leading international pawn shop operator with 3,300+ locations across the U.S., UK, and Latin America, offering pawn loans, retail sales, and gold buying services.
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the leading international operator of pawn stores with over 3,300 retail locations spanning 29 U.S. states, the District of Columbia, the United Kingdom, and Latin America (Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador). The company was founded to serve cash and credit-constrained consumers who need immediate liquidity or alternative retail access.

FirstCash operates a traditional pawn business model: customers can pledge personal property (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, musical instruments, and other merchandise) to receive non-recourse pawn loans secured by the pledged items. Beyond lending, FirstCash buys and sells merchandise, specializes in gold/precious metals purchasing, and offers layaway services with 10% down payments. Through its wholly-owned subsidiary AFF, the company also provides lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions across a network of 15,000+ retail merchant partners.

FirstCash's scale and geographic reach distinguish it from independent pawn operators. With approximately 22,000 employees and inclusion in both the S&P MidCap 400 Index and Russell 2000 Index, it operates as a publicly traded, professionally managed enterprise rather than a small local shop. The company maintains standardized operations, store locator technology, and consistent inventory across locations, reducing the unpredictability often associated with independent pawnbrokers.

As a pawn lender, FirstCash serves consumers facing temporary cash needs or lacking traditional credit access, but pawn loans come with the inherent trade-off of surrendering collateral and the risk of item loss if loans aren't repaid. While faster and easier to access than bank loans, pawn financing is expensive relative to traditional personal loans and should be considered a last-resort option for short-term cash needs only.
address: 2905 E Arkansas Ln #106, Grand Prairie, TX 75051

company_info.headquarters: 2905 E Arkansas Ln #106, Grand Prairie, TX 75051

pros: Nationwide network of 3,300+ locations across 29 states plus DC, UK, and Latin America for convenient access
Fast cash on-the-spot for pawn loans and gold/precious metals sales with no credit check required
Non-recourse loans—borrowers have no obligation to repay if unable, only lose pledged item
Publicly traded company (S&P MidCap 400, Russell 2000) providing institutional stability and regulatory oversight
Layaway service available with only 10% down for customers wanting to purchase merchandise gradually
Wide variety of items accepted (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, instruments, sporting goods)
No credit score requirement—accessible to credit-constrained consumers excluded from traditional lending
cons: Pawn loans are expensive relative to personal loans; interest rates and fees can be substantially higher than bank alternatives
Risk of losing collateral permanently if unable to repay loan—items are sold after redemption period expires
Limited loan amounts tied to item value; unsuitable for large cash needs requiring $5,000+
Redemption periods are typically 30-90 days, creating time pressure to repay or lose property
Merchandise selection is unpredictable and varies by location; inventory depends on what customers pawn
best_for: Cash-constrained consumers needing immediate funds ($100-$2,000) without credit qualification
Individuals with poor or no credit history unable to access traditional personal loans or credit lines
People with valuable jewelry, electronics, or other collateral willing to pledge items for short-term loans
Bargain hunters and second-hand shoppers seeking discounted merchandise at pawn retail locations
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for credit-constrained consumers facing urgent short-term cash needs ($100-$2,000) who have collateral they're willing to pledge and cannot qualify for traditional loans. The main caveat is that pawn lending is expensive, carries the risk of losing your property permanently if unable to repay, and should only be used as a last resort for temporary emergencies, not ongoing financing.
services: Pawn loans secured by pledged personal property (non-recourse, 30-90 day terms)
Gold, silver, and precious metals buying—cash payment on-the-spot
Retail sales of used and new merchandise (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, instruments, sporting goods)
Layaway service with 10% down payment and flexible payment terms
Store locator tool to find nearest FirstCash location
Online inventory browsing and product search
Lease-to-own and retail finance solutions through AFF subsidiary (15,000+ merchant partners)
Consumer FAQs and educational content about pawn lending process
meta_description: FirstCash operates 3,300+ pawn shops across the U.S., UK, and Latin America, offering loans, retail sales, and gold buying.
